Farn PolrrrcAL Pnncrlcps CoMMrssroN
-128 J Street r Suire 620 r Sacramento. CA 9-5gl.t-2329
(916) 322--s660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 30. 2008
Elena Chavez, City Clerk
City of San Femando
I l7 MacNeil Street
San Fernando, CA 91340-2993
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-163
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through gl014. All
statutory references are to the Government code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulation-s of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections I 8 I l0 throulh I 8997 of Title 2 of theCalifornia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Divisioi 6 of the california code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
Dear Ms. Chavez:
This letter is in response to your request regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the ..Act',). I
QUESTION
May the committees involved in a January 13,2}}g,special recall election
combine the semi-annual statement with the second pre-electibn statement due in
connection with the election?
CONCLUSION
Committees involved in the January 13,2}}9,election should file according to the
enclosed schedule, ryh]ch will satisfy the campaign reporting requirements for candidates
and committees participating in the election.


File No. A-08-163
Page No. 2
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two pre-election campaign statements disclosing contributions received
and expenditures made in connection with that election. These pre-election campaign
statements are generally filed 40 days before the election and again 12 days befoie the
election. (Section 84200.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed J*u*y 3 i and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when an
overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of the semi-annual statement would be an
additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.) In most
cases, because Section 82018(a) provides that the "cumulative amount" of contributions
received and expenditures made by candidates and committees is calculated on a calendar
year basis (January I through December 3l), a pre-election filing is not combined with the
semi-annual statement covering through December 3l of the prior year. However, Section
82018 provides that the cumulative amount of contributions rlceiv.a *a expenditures
made can be calculated over more than one calendar year under specified ciicumstances
such as when a pre-election statement is filed in one calendar year for an election held in
the following calendar year. (Section 82018(b).) In your situation, both pre-election
statements cover reporting periods in 2008 while the election will be held in 2009.
Thus, in this case, filing the two pre-election statements in connection with the
January l3 election will satisft the January 3l,2}}g,semi-annual filing requirement. A
campaign statement filing schedule which outlines the due dates for filing statements is
enclosed for your convenience.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
ANALYSIS
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counselt
/.r,1 ,t- (*r^l
By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
Cwjgl
